name: Bone a Fidos
recipeIngredient: 2 cups cracked wheat OR 2 tablespoons milk
1/2 cup nonfat dry milk (lightly spooned into the cup)
recipeInstructions:
Equipment: Cookie sheets lined with parchment or aluminum foil; rolling pin; 3-3.5 inch bone cutter or 2.5 inch round cookie cutter. Place 2 oven racks in the upper and lower thirds of the oven. Preheat oven to 300°F.
1. Sprinkle dry yeast over warm water (110°F if dry yeast, 100°F if compressed yeast). Add sugar and let sit in a draft-free spot for 10-20 minutes. The mixture should be full of bubbles. If not, discard yeast as it's too old.
2. In a large bowl, mix all dry ingredients. Stir in the yeast mixture and 3 cups broth. Mix with hands until smooth and supple, adding more broth if needed.
3. Knead half the dough on a lightly floured surface for a few seconds. Cover remaining dough with a damp towel.
4. Roll out dough to an 18 x 13 x 1/4 inch rectangle. Cut into desired shapes using bone cutter or cookie cutter. Re-roll scraps and repeat with remaining dough.
5. Mix egg and milk for glaze. Brush on cookies. Bake for 45-60 minutes until brown and firm, rotating sheets halfway through.
6. Cool cookies on wire racks before storing in an airtight container at room temperature. The dough must be used immediately after mixing.
